name: Greenland (Denmark)

colors.symbolism: the ice cap, the glaciers and the snow that cover most of Greenland
the sun, the ocean, the fjords and the country's ties to Denmark (whose flag is also red and white)

description: Two equal horizontal bands — white above red — with a large disc straddling the boundary, offset toward the hoist; the disc's upper half is red against the white band, and its lower half is white against the red band.
history: Designed by Greenlandic artist Thue Christiansen, the flag was selected from over 555 submissions in a 1985 competition and adopted on 21 June 1985 (the summer solstice). It is called Erfalasorput ('our flag'). It is the only flag of a Nordic country that does not feature a Nordic cross.

features.symbolism: the white upper half represents the ice cap and glaciers; the red lower half represents the ocean, the fjords, and the setting sun reflected on the sea

features.role: lower (red) half of a disc on the white field
features.symbolism: the setting sun half-submerged in the sea; the disc straddles the dividing line, its upper half red on white and its lower half white on red

features.role: upper (white) half of the disc on the red field
features.symbolism: the iceberg or the sun rising/setting over the sea, with the colours reversed against the red lower band
